sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) computed at the GFN2-xTB level (semi-empirical tight-binding, xtb backend) in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Fukui indices were derived from finite differences of Mulliken partial charges on the neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) states. Geometry was used as supplied (no re-optimization). The headline value reported is f⁺(Fe) = 0.22885456, the largest electrophilic Fukui index. Iron (atom 11) is simultaneously the most electrophilic (f⁺ = 0.22885) and most nucleophilic (f⁻ = 0.25938) site, with a negative dual descriptor (−0.03053) indicating net nucleophilic character. All Cp ring carbons (~0.01364 f⁺, ~0.01218 f⁻) and hydrogens (~0.06348 f⁺, ~0.06188 f⁻) are symmetrically equivalent and show much smaller Fukui weights. Integrity: trustworthy = true; sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000.
